## [Cyberpunk 2077] VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after view map pressure on NVIDIA 580.x
[...]
Cyberpunk 2077 crashes consistently with VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST on NVIDIA 580.x driver after 5-45 minutes of gameplay. The crash appears related to descriptor heap pressure ("Intense view map pressure" in logs). Hardware is confirmed stable via stress testing. Multiple workarounds attempted without success.
[...]
- Arch Linux, kernel 6.17.9-zen
- NVIDIA RTX 2080, driver 580.105.08
- Ryzen 9 9950X3D, 96GB RAM
- Proton Experimental / GE-Proton (both affected)
[...]
Game freezes with VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after variable playtime (5-45 minutes depending on settings). GPU utilization drops from 99% to 1%.
[...]
Intense view map pressure, recreating view maps (2048 -> 3072)
vkd3d_queue_wait_idle: vkQueueWaitIdle failed, vr -4 (VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST)
[...]
### Tested workarounds
[...]
- VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv (best result, ~45min before crash)
- VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_0
- PROTON_HEAP_DELAY_FREE=1
- PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=0
- Ray Tracing OFF, lowest graphics settings
- Driver downgrade failed (DKMS incompatible with kernel 6.17).
[...]
**GPU stress test**: gpu-burn 5 minutes, 0 errors - hardware is stable.
[...]
> > Intense view map pressure
> 
> This is completely benign. And 3k is nothing here. It's expected when RT is enabled.
[...]
> Looking at the logs more closely, the crash happens ~14 seconds after "Intense view map pressure! Got 5120 views". Between the warning and the crash, there's no other error - just QueryInterface warnings which seem unrelated.
> 
> Timeline:
> 
> - 1773.845: "Intense view map pressure! Got 5120 views"
> - 1788.417: "Failed to submit queue(s), vr -4" → VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST
> 
> This was with RT OFF and lowest settings. Is 5120 views still considered normal?
> 
> I can provide dmesg/kernel logs from the crash if that would help identify the driver-side failure.
[...]
> Additional findings from further testing:
> 
> ### No kernel errors
> 
> No kernel/dmesg errors during the crash. The GPU device lost happens purely at Vulkan/userspace level - the NVIDIA kernel driver (580.105.08) doesn't report any fault or timeout.
> 
> ### System-wide freeze
> 
> The freeze affects the entire desktop, not just the game process. Mouse/keyboard become unresponsive fo
Source: [Cyberpunk 2077] VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after view map pressure on NVIDIA 580.x · Issue #2756 · HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton (https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2756)

## VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST in Cyberpunk 2077 photo mode with GPU descriptor heap enabled
[...]
Cyberpunk 2077 hangs with VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST when taking a photo in photo mode while GPU descriptor heap support is enabled via PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1. The game is 100% stable during normal gameplay both with and without the descriptor heap support enabled; the crash is specific to pressing spacebar to capture a photo in photo mode with heap support enabled.
[...]
| Component | Details |
| --- | --- |
| **CPU** | AMD Ryzen 9 9950X 16-Core (32 threads) |
| **GPU** | NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 |
| **Driver** | NVIDIA 595.71.05 |
| **OS** | CachyOS |
| **Kernel** | 7.0.2-2-cachyos (x86_64) |
[...]
The crash reproduces across
[...]
proton-cachyos releases & vkd3d-proton builds, including:
[...]
- cachyos-10.0-20260420-slr / Proton Experimental 10.0-20260407 / vkd3d-proton 2889f1c8
- cachyos-11.0-20260428-slr / Proton Experimental 11.0-20260428 / vkd3d-proton 790cfc68
[...]
1. Install Cyberpunk 2077
2. Use proton-cachyos (tested with `cachyos-10.0-20260420-slr` and `cachyos-11.0-20260428-slr`)
3. Set launch options / environment: `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1`
4. Launch game, load a save, enter photo mode
5. Press spacebar to take a photo
6. Graphics hang immediately — display freezes, audio continues playing
[...]
```
May 02 13:34:54 cachyzen kernel: NVRM: Xid (PCI:0000:01:00): 31, pid=271667, name=Cyberpunk2077.e, channel 0x00000044, intr 00000000. MMU Fault: ENGINE GRAPHICS HUBCLIENT_FE faulted @ 0x0_0aca1000. Fault is of type FAULT_PTE ACCESS_TYPE_VIRT_WRITE
```
[...]
Maybe related: shortly before the crash, the log shows repeated `ClearUnorderedAccessViewUint` calls with heap invalidation warnings:
[...]
```
87018.032: d3d12_command_list_ClearUnorderedAccessViewUint: ClearUAVUint called without active heap.
87018.032: d3d12_command_list_invalidate_descriptor_heap: Invalidating descriptor heap due to meta command which is incompatible with heaps.
```
[...]
Then the hang:
[...]
```
87018.180: d3d12_device_mark_as_removed: Device 0000000129330080 is lost (reason 0x887a0005, "VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST").
87018.180: vkd3d_memory_transfer_queue_flush_locked: Failed to submit command buffer, vr -4.
87018.180: d3d12_device_mark_as_removed: Device 0000000129330080 is lost (reason 0x80004005, "Failed to execute pending memory clears.").
87018.181: d3d1
Source: VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST in Cyberpunk 2077 photo mode with GPU descriptor heap enabled · Issue #2979 · HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton (https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2979)

## RTX 5090 often freezes presentation for 3-5 seconds in certain games
[...]
CachyOS (Arch Linux)
[...]
### Hardware:
[...]
GPU 0: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (UUID: GPU-addf70ec-1a4e-f8db-39bc-59d1ab271798)
[...]
### Describe the bug
[...]
When playing certain titles on my RTX 5090 rig, I experience frequent "freezes" where the game window will freeze but some audio continues to play in the background. During the freezes, I am able to use everything else on my PC normally without any indications of a GPU hang. After 3-5 seconds, the game "unfreezes" and then catches up to where it should be.
[...]
This happens with every compositor I have tried: KWin wayland, cosmic-comp, and gamescope. I have tried every 570 and 575 driver release and experience this issue on all of them.
[...]
Games I experience this freezing with include:
[...]
- Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 (DX12)
- The First Berserker: Khazan (DX11 and DX12)
- Star Citizen (DX11)
- Final Fantasy VII: Rebirth (DX12)
[...]
Games I **do not** experience this freezing with include:
[...]
- Kingdom Come: Deliverance (DX11)
- Horizon: Forbidden West (DX12)
- Death Stranding
[...]
### To Reproduce
[...]
KWin repro steps:
[...]
1. Launch Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 on an RTX 5090 (settings should not matter)
2. Play the game for 5-20 minutes
[...]
Gamescope repro steps:
[...]
1. Launch Steam inside of gamescope with `gamescope -e -f -h -w -r --mangoapp --adaptive-sync -- steam -steamdeck -steamos3 -steampal -gamepadui`
2. Launch Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 (make sure the only launch option you are using is `SteamDeck=0 %command%`)
3. Play the game for 5-20 minutes
4. When you encounter a freeze, try pulling up one of the Steam side menus with the Xbox logo or Shift + Tab on your keyboard
Source: RTX 5090 often freezes presentation for 3-5 seconds in certain games · Issue #880 · NVIDIA/open-gpu-kernel-modules (https://github.com/NVIDIA/open-gpu-kernel-modules/issues/880)

## Cyberpunk 2077 - Memory leak to OOM with DXR
[...]
- Author: [@l-t-s](https://github.com/l-t-s)
- State: open
- Created: 2025-07-20T16:38:11Z
- Updated: 2025-09-04T00:18:21Z
[...]
I found that by setting `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr`, Cyberpunk would no longer consume all of system memory and hang the thread on close.
[...]
I was attempting to play Cyberpunk 2077, which I had bought from GOG, via Lutris. Starting the game worked fine and remained relatively stable for a few minutes. A moment later the game would freeze and my whole system with it, until the OOM killer would kick in to start shooting down whatever processes I had open.
[...]
I observed the game's resource usage and saw it would immediately jump to around 6GB RAM usage on the intro, slowly increasing to around 8GB in the main menu, and finally when loading into a save it would be around 10GB from where it would over time increase to fill my full 32GB RAM and 16GB pagefile, freezing the system and activating the OOM killer.
[...]
So I tried a variety of things: different kernels (zen, lts, cachyos), GE-Proton and Wine versions available on Lutris, (de)activating some additional features and changing game settings.
[...]
None of those had an effect and the result was the same. I later faced the same issue on HeroicLauncher.
[...]
Eventually I tried different versions of the Lutris runner libraries for Wine, and I found VKD3D 2.10 ran perfectly fine; Cyberpunk launched to around 2GB RAM usage, climbed to 4GB loading into a save, and hovered well under 6GB for hours without issue.
[...]
Checking the release notes for VKD3D 2.11 I saw that DXR became the default option, so I disabled it with the `nodxr` option, and it worked.
[...]
I do not know what that feature is, what it is meant to do or why it causes the game to consume all memory and hang the thread, but I'm quite sure the issue stems from its interaction with something on my system.
[...]
Cyberpunk 2077 from GOG, downloaded and managed using Lutris and HeroicLauncher.
[...]
- GPU: Intel B580
- Driver: mesa-tkg-git 25.2.0_devel.208628.3c7a834ebc7-1 with KMS xe driver
- Wine version: GE-Proton and Wine versions up to Wine 10.8 that I tried
- VKD3D-Proton version: 2.14, 2.13, 2.12, 2.11.1, 2.11 with DXR on by default
[...]
[dxr_ge-proton-10.10_logs.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21335961/dxr_ge-proton-10.
[...]
_logs.tar.gz)
[...]
This first is with DXR enabled, few minutes in-game, and having to SIGKILL the process after quitting.

[nodxr_ge-proton-10.10_logs.tar.gz](
Source: Cyberpunk 2077 - Memory leak to OOM with DXR · Issue #2549 · HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton (https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2549)

summary.text:
# Cyberpunk 2077 Freezing on Linux: Summary

## Key Issues Found

### **VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST Crashes (Most Common)**
Multiple users report **system-wide freezes** with `VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST` errors on NVIDIA GPUs, particularly:
- **NVIDIA 580.x drivers**: Crashes after 5–45 minutes of gameplay
- **NVIDIA 595.x drivers**: Photo mode crashes when GPU descriptor heap is enabled (`PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1`)
- **Hardware confirmed stable** via stress testing; crashes appear driver/vkd3d-related

**Symptoms:**
- GPU utilization drops from 99% to 1%
- Entire desktop freezes (mouse/keyboard unresponsive)
- No kernel-level errors logged
- Audio may continue briefly

### **Descriptor Heap Issues**
- Crashes correlate with "Intense view map pressure" and descriptor heap operations
- Black screen crashes reported across multiple games when `VK_EXT_descriptor_heap` is enabled
- Feature is still experimental (hidden behind `VKD3D_CONFIG=descriptor_heap`)
- Multiple known NVIDIA bugs outstanding before stabilization

### **Complete Launch Failures**
Some users cannot launch the game at all:
- Black screen on startup with ~40% CPU usage
- `VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST` during initialization
- Affects various Proton versions (7.0–Experimental)

### **Memory Leak (DXR-Related)**
- DXR enabled by default in vkd3d 2.11+ causes memory exhaustion
- Workaround: `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr`

### **Overlay/Input Issues**
- Steam overlay blocking all UI input in some Proton versions (GE-10-13+)
- CET overlay eating invalid gamepad keybinds
- Disabling Steam overlay fixes the issue

## Attempted Workarounds (Limited Success)
- `VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv` (delays crash by ~10 min)
- `VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_0`
- `PROTON_HEAP_DELAY_FREE=1`
- `PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=0`
- Disabling ray tracing & lowering graphics

**None reliably prevent crashes**—suggests underlying driver instability rather than game settings.

## Current Status
The descriptor heap rebase (PR #2943) is in development but explicitly notes **"several NVIDIA bugs left to resolve"** before production use. Issues remain open with no confirmed fixes.

---

## Sources
1. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2756 (descriptor heap device lost)
2. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2979 (photo mode hang)
3. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2872 (black screen tracker)
4. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2549 (DXR memory leak)
5. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/pull/2943 (descriptor heap rebase)

urls.title: [Cyberpunk 2077] VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after view map pressure on NVIDIA 580.x
urls.content:
Cyberpunk 2077 crashes consistently with VK\_ERROR\_DEVICE\_LOST on NVIDIA 580.x driver after 5-45 minutes of gameplay. The crash appears related to descriptor heap pressure ("Intense view map pressure" in logs). Hardware is confirmed stable via stress testing. Multiple workarounds attempted without success.

### Software information

Cyberpunk 2077 (Steam, AppID 1091500)

### System information

*   Arch Linux, kernel 6.17.9-zen
*   NVIDIA RTX 2080, driver 580.105.08
*   Ryzen 9 9950X3D, 96GB RAM
*   Proton Experimental / GE-Proton (both affected)

### Problem

Game freezes with VK\_ERROR\_DEVICE\_LOST after variable playtime (5-45 minutes depending on settings). GPU utilization drops from 99% to 1%.

### Log shows

Intense view map pressure, recreating view maps (2048 -> 3072)  
vkd3d\_queue\_wait\_idle: vkQueueWaitIdle failed, vr -4 (VK\_ERROR\_DEVICE\_LOST)

### Tested workarounds (all still crash eventually):

*   VKD3D\_CONFIG=no\_upload\_hvv (best result, ~45min before crash)
*   VKD3D\_FEATURE\_LEVEL=12\_0
*   PROTON\_HEAP\_DELAY\_FREE=1
*   PROTON\_ENABLE\_NVAPI=0
*   Ray Tracing OFF, lowest graphics settings
*   Driver downgrade failed (DKMS incompatible with kernel 6.17).

**GPU stress test**: gpu-burn 5 minutes, 0 errors - hardware is stable.

[steam-1091500.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24190749/steam-1091500.log)

Cyberpunk 2077

Chromebook Ready Status

content.text:
Cyberpunk 2077 crashes consistently with VK\_ERROR\_DEVICE\_LOST on NVIDIA 580.x driver after 5-45 minutes of gameplay. The crash appears related to descriptor heap pressure ("Intense view map pressure" in logs). Hardware is confirmed stable via stress testing. Multiple workarounds attempted without success.

### Software information

Cyberpunk 2077 (Steam, AppID 1091500)

### System information

*   Arch Linux, kernel 6.17.9-zen
*   NVIDIA RTX 2080, driver 580.105.08
*   Ryzen 9 9950X3D, 96GB RAM
*   Proton Experimental / GE-Proton (both affected)

### Problem

Game freezes with VK\_ERROR\_DEVICE\_LOST after variable playtime (5-45 minutes depending on settings). GPU utilization drops from 99% to 1%.

### Log shows

Intense view map pressure, recreating view maps (2048 -> 3072)  
vkd3d\_queue\_wait\_idle: vkQueueWaitIdle failed, vr -4 (VK\_ERROR\_DEVICE\_LOST)

### Tested workarounds (all still crash eventually):

*   VKD3D\_CONFIG=no\_upload\_hvv (best result, ~45min before crash)
*   VKD3D\_FEATURE\_LEVEL=12\_0
*   PROTON\_HEAP\_DELAY\_FREE=1
*   PROTON\_ENABLE\_NVAPI=0
*   Ray Tracing OFF, lowest graphics settings
*   Driver downgrade failed (DKMS incompatible with kernel 6.17).

**GPU stress test**: gpu-burn 5 minutes, 0 errors - hardware is stable.

[steam-1091500.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24190749/steam-1091500.log)

details.title: [Cyberpunk 2077] VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after view map pressure on NVIDIA 580.x
details.prompt: What is the current status and workarounds for this VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST / descriptor heap freeze issue with Cyberpunk 2077 on NVIDIA? Are there any confirmed fixes? What env vars help?

DXVK_CONFIG_FILE="/home/swarn/dox/games/dlss/dxvk.conf" WINEDLLOVERRIDES="dxgi=n,b" PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1 LOW_LATENCY_LAYER_REFLEX=1 gamemoderun mangohud %command% --launcher-skip -skipStartScreen

My sudo master-gamemode --on is a custom script do not add that to your launch command or the end with sudo master-gamemode --off, as those do nothing on anyone elses system but mine. the master game mode is for my naga trinity to switch from deveice mode and to double check the mitigation lock is toggled off from the standard gamemoderun command.

I turned off motion blur for improved performance.

Difficult To Read Text:

Some of the map UI in-game and the car names when entering a vehicle are difficult to read.

Save Games:Other

It sometimes takes ages to load your saves in the menu.

# Issue: HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton #2756
[...]
## [Cyberpunk 2077] VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after view map pressure on NVIDIA 580.x
[...]
- Author: [@Heaven671](https://github.com/Heaven671)
- State: open
- Created: 2025-12-16T12:01:44Z
- Updated: 2025-12-16T14:12:49Z
[...]
Cyberpunk 2077 crashes consistently with VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST on NVIDIA 580.x driver after 5-45 minutes of gameplay. The crash appears related to descriptor heap pressure ("Intense view map pressure" in logs). Hardware is confirmed stable via stress testing. Multiple workarounds attempted without success.
[...]
- Arch Linux, kernel 6.17.9-zen
- NVIDIA RTX 2080, driver 580.105.08
- Ryzen 9 9950X3D, 96GB RAM
- Proton Experimental / GE-Proton (both affected)
[...]
Game freezes with VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after variable playtime (5-45 minutes depending on settings). GPU utilization drops from 99% to 1%.
[...]
Intense view map pressure, recreating view maps (2048 -> 3072)
vkd3d_queue_wait_idle: vkQueueWaitIdle failed, vr -4 (VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST)
[...]
### Tested workarounds (all still crash eventually):

- VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv (best result, ~45min before crash)
- VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_0
- PROTON_HEAP_DELAY_FREE=1
- PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=0
- Ray Tracing OFF, lowest graphics settings
- Driver downgrade failed (DKMS incompatible with kernel 6.17).
[...]
**GPU stress test**: gpu-burn 5 minutes, 0 errors - hardware is stable.
[...]
> > Intense view map pressure
> 
> This is completely benign. And 3k is nothing here. It's expected when RT is enabled.
[...]
> Looking at the logs more closely, the crash happens ~14 seconds after "Intense view map pressure! Got 5120 views". Between the warning and the crash, there's no other error - just QueryInterface warnings which seem unrelated.
> 
> Timeline:
> 
> - 1773.845: "Intense view map pressure! Got 5120 views"
> - 1788.417: "Failed to submit queue(s), vr -4" → VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST
> 
> This was with RT OFF and lowest settings. Is 5120 views still considered normal?
> 
> I can provide dmesg/kernel logs from the crash if that would help identify the driver-side failure.
[...]
> Additional findings from further testing:
> 
> ### No kernel errors
> 
> No kernel/dmesg errors during the crash. The GPU device lost happens purely at Vulkan/userspace level - the NVIDIA kernel driver (580.105.08) doesn't report any fault or timeout.
> 
> ### System-wide freeze
> 
> The freeze affects the entire desktop, not just the game process. Mouse/keyboard become unresponsive fo

## NVIDIA: DX12 games eventually freeze/crash unless VKD3D shader cache is disabled (VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0)
[...]
- Author: [@AreebDurrani](https://github.com/AreebDurrani)
- State: open
- Created: 2026-01-12T17:31:49Z
- Updated: 2026-01-19T17:20:48Z
[...]
On NVIDIA GPUs, DX12 games using VKD3D-Proton will eventually freeze or crash after some time unless the VKD3D shader cache is disabled via:

VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0

With the shader cache disabled, the freezes/crashes no longer occur (at the cost of increased shader compilation stutter).
[...]
This issue is not related to DLSS or Frame Generation. It reproduces: In games with no DLSS support, whether DLSS is enabled or disabled, and across multiple GPUs
[...]
**Software environment:**

Driver: NVIDIA Linux driver 590.48.01-1

VKD3D-Proton: 3.0b

Wine: 10.20 staging

Kernel: 6.18.3-zen1-1-zen

Session: Wayland

Compositor: KDE
[...]
Start a DX12 game via Proton, play normally. After some amount of time (varies by game; typically minutes to an hour), the game will:
[...]
Freeze permanently, or
[...]
Crash without producing a useful error message
[...]
**Expected behavior:**
[...]
Games should continue running indefinitely without freezing or crashing.
[...]
**Actual behavior:**
Games eventually freeze or crash unless the VKD3D shader cache is disabled.
[...]
**Workaround:**
[...]
Disabling the VKD3D shader cache fully prevents the issue:

VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0

This workaround is reliable across all tested systems and games, but introduces shader compilation stutter.
[...]
This lack of diagnostics is part of what makes the issue difficult to debug, but the shader-cache workaround consistently avoids the problem.
[...]
This issue is independent of DLSS / Frame Generation

It reproduces even in games that do not contain DLSS code paths
[...]
The fact that disabling the shader cache alone resolves the issue suggests a possible interaction between:
[...]
VKD3D pipeline/shader cache handling
[...]
NVIDIA driver behavior (590.xx branch)
[...]
**@OneShot95** commented · Jan 19, 2026 at 5:20pm
[...]
> On Nvidia drivers - 590.48.01, this issue is still present.
Source: NVIDIA: DX12 games eventually freeze/crash unless VKD3D shader cache is disabled (VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0) · Issue #2793 · HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton (https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2793)

## VK_EXT_descriptor_heap (PR #2805) fixes Xid 109 Hard Crashes + Crimson Desert now may work on the 595 drivers for RTX 5000 cards (Blackwell)
[...]
- Author: [@ThomasEricB](https://github.com/ThomasEricB)
- State: open
- Reactions: 👍 4
- Created: 2026-03-27T17:56:04Z
- Updated: 2026-04-02T03:36:32Z
[...]
Crimson Desert completely crashes during loading on Blackwell cards with the latest 595 drivers. @NVIDIA internally tracked the issue as ["6017526 Crimson Desert still soft locks upon completing shader compilation part of loading (field of cubes) with driver 595.58.03"](https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/595-release-feedback-discussion/362561/228).
[...]
HOWEVER, using Proton-CachyOS's compiled https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/pull/2805 ("Prototype implementation of VK_EXT_descriptor_heap") with the flag `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1` **the game now works**! See logs below with the flag and without the flag turned on.
[...]
Also might be the fix for the tree issue as reported by me and others on the Proton Github (I don't know if it is the latest drivers or VK_EXT_descriptor_heap): https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/9595#issuecomment-4141966615 . Tree artifacts completely gone in 595 drivers + VK_EXT_descriptor_heap.
[...]
**EDIT:** There are many reports below + on Reddit and on Discord that using the VK_EXT_descriptor_heap branch also fixes Xid 109 Hard Crashes. Since it has been massively reported to me by many people, I decided to incorporate this into this issue.
[...]
**EDIT 2:** Conversely, there is a minority of people that this workaround didn't work for them in Crimson Desert right away. I suspect that their game may be left in bad state after trying to run the game without this workaround first. All that I can suggest to people who have been struggling is to clean their steam cache and verify their game files. This shader cleanup has been reported to solve these issues with the workaround most of the time. However, If that still doesn't work, all I can think of is to backup their save and reinstall the game. For solving shader issues, I just do (always double check the file paths using `rm -rf` commands!):
[...]
```
rm -rf ~/.cache/nvidia/GLCache/*
rm -rf ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/shadercache/3321460/
rm -rf ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Crimson\ Desert/bin64/vkd3d-proton.cache
rm -rf ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Crimson\ Desert/bin64/vkd3d-proton.cache.write
```
Source: VK_EXT_descriptor_heap (PR #2805) fixes Xid 109 Hard Crashes + Crimson Desert now may work on the 595 drivers for RTX 5000 cards (Blackwell) · Issue #2914 · HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton (https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2914)

Thank you and everyone else who reported the crash with Forza Horizon 6. We have root caused the issue and will ship a fix in an upcoming driver release. The bug for tracking this issue is 6181953.
[...]
Subnautica 2 seems to refuse to start on rtx 50 series cards. When you push play, you will see the loading screen for about 5 seconds before it crashes.
[...]
I was able to get a successful bootup by setting the proton version to experimental with these launch arguments VKD3D_DISABLE_EXTENSIONS=VK_EXT_mesh_shader,VK_NV_raw_access_chains %command%
[...]
50
[...]
(no startup)
[...]
Tested with RTX 3080 (starts up fine)
[...]
fresh installation of Windrose (Steam) crashes on startup using nvidia 595.x on an RTX5080. several Proton versions tested.
[...]
Downgrading to 580 or 590 fixes the crash and no issues are seen.
[...]
I can’t boot with Kernel 7.0+ with R595 driver (I tried 595.71.05, 595.58.03 and 595.44.07) using the .run file.
[...]
I boot with Kernel 7.0.7 (and previous versions) in multi-user mode (no X environment), installl the driver .run (compile modules, install driver and 32bit libraries, rebuild initramfs) and reboot.
[...]
From now, is impossible boot with Kernel 7.0+. I have the journalctl -b -k. Final lines:
[...]
I attached the full log.
[...]
The only fix to my situation is boot with kernel 6.19.12.
Source: 595 release feedback & discussion - Page 22 - Linux - NVIDIA Developer Forums (https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/595-release-feedback-discussion/362561?page=22)

## Cyberpunk 2077 - Memory leak to OOM with DXR
[...]
I found that by setting `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr`, Cyberpunk would no longer consume all of system memory and hang the thread on close.
[...]
I was attempting to play Cyberpunk 2077, which I had bought from GOG, via Lutris. Starting the game worked fine and remained relatively stable for a few minutes. A moment later the game would freeze and my whole system with it, until the OOM killer would kick in to start shooting down whatever processes I had open.
[...]
I observed the game's resource usage and saw it would immediately jump to around 6GB RAM usage on the intro, slowly increasing to around 8GB in the main menu, and finally when loading into a save it would be around 10GB from where it would over time increase to fill my full 32GB RAM and 16GB pagefile, freezing the system and activating the OOM killer.
[...]
Eventually I tried different versions of the Lutris runner libraries for Wine, and I found VKD3D 2.10 ran perfectly fine; Cyberpunk launched to around 2GB RAM usage, climbed to 4GB loading into a save, and hovered well under 6GB for hours without issue.
[...]
Checking the release notes for VKD3D 2.11 I saw that DXR became the default option, so I disabled it with the `nodxr` option, and it worked.
[...]
I do not know what that feature is, what it is meant to do or why it causes the game to consume all memory and hang the thread, but I'm quite sure the issue stems from its interaction with something on my system.
[...]
, all current

content.text:
# NVIDIA Driver Stability Issues with VKD3D-Proton (2025-2026)

## Overview
Multiple critical stability issues affect DX12 games running through VKD3D-Proton on NVIDIA GPUs, particularly with driver versions 580.x, 590.x, and 595.x. Issues range from device loss crashes to system-wide freezes.

## Key Issues

### 1. **VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST Crashes (Multiple Driver Versions)**
- **Affected:** Cyberpunk 2077, other DX12 games
- **Drivers:** 580.x, 595.x
- **Symptom:** Game freezes/crashes after 5-45 minutes (Cyberpunk); system becomes unresponsive; GPU utilization drops to 1%
- **Root cause:** Related to descriptor heap pressure and view map recreation; occurs at Vulkan userspace level with no kernel errors reported
- **Status:** Issue #2756 (open, reported 2025-12-16)

### 2. **DX12 Shader Cache Freeze/Crash Bug (590.x)**
- **Affected:** Multiple DX12 titles, including non-DLSS games
- **Drivers:** 590.48.01 confirmed; affects 590.xx branch
- **Symptom:** Games freeze or crash after variable playtime (minutes to hours)
- **Workaround:** `VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0` (disables crash but increases shader compilation stutter)
- **Status:** Issue #2793 (open, reported 2026-01-12)
- **Note:** Suggests interaction between VKD3D cache handling and NVIDIA driver behavior

### 3. **Xid 109 Hard Crashes & Game Load Failures (595.x)**
- **Affected:** Crimson Desert (Blackwell/RTX 5000 cards), other titles
- **Drivers:** 595.x (595.58.03, 595.71.05 confirmed)
- **Symptom:** Hard crashes during shader compilation or gameplay; soft locks
- **Potential fix:** `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1` flag with VK_EXT_descriptor_heap implementation (PR #2805)
- **Status:** Widely reported; fix in testing phase
- **Caveat:** Some users report cache corruption requiring shader cleanup

### 4. **Cyberpunk 2077 DXR Memory Leak**
- **Drivers:** Affects VKD3D 2.11+ (DXR enabled by default)
- **Symptom:** System memory consumption climbs to 32GB+, triggering OOM killer; system freeze
- **Workaround:** `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr` resolves the issue
- **Status:** Issue #2549 (open)

### 5. **Photo Mode Hang (595.x with Descriptor Heap)**
- **Affected:** Cyberpunk 2077
- **Drivers:** 595.71.05
- **Symptom:** VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST when taking photos in photo mode with `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1`
- **Status:** Issue #2979 (open, reported 2026-05-02)

## Recommended Workarounds (By Symptom)

| Issue | Workaround | Trade-offs |
|-------|-----------|-----------|
| General DX12 freezes (590.x) | `VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0` | Shader compilation stutter |
| Xid 109 crashes (595.x) | `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1` | May break photo mode; cache corruption possible |
| DXR memory leak | `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr` | Disables ray tracing features |
| Descriptor heap issues | `VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv` | Limited effectiveness (~45min extension in Cyberpunk) |
| Specific Subnautica 2 crash (RTX 50-series) | `VKD3D_DISABLE_EXTENSIONS=VK_EXT_mesh_shader,VK_NV_raw_access_chains` | Feature degradation |

## Caveats & Unresolved Items

- **Hardware stability confirmed:** Stress tests show GPU is stable; issues are driver/VKD3D interaction
- **Multiple unresolved paths:** No single fix works across all affected games/configurations
- **Descriptor heap workaround side effects:** Some users report it fixes some crashes but breaks others (photo mode)
- **Kernel compatibility:** 595.x drivers have boot issues with Kernel 7.0+ on some systems; Kernel 6.19.x workaround reported
- **Shader cache cleanup needed:** Using descriptor heap workaround may require manual cache clearing (`~/.cache/nvidia/GLCache/*`, Steam shadercache)

## Sources

1. [Cyberpunk 2077] VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after view map pressure on NVIDIA 580.x — https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2756
2. NVIDIA: DX12 games eventually freeze/crash unless VKD3D shader cache is disabled — https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2793
3. VK_EXT_descriptor_heap (PR #2805) fixes Xid 109 Hard Crashes — https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2914
4. Cyberpunk 2077 - Memory leak to OOM with DXR — https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2549
5. VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST in Cyberpunk 2077 photo mode with GPU descriptor heap enabled — https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2979
6. NVIDIA 595 Drivers release feedback & discussion — https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/595-release-feedback-discussion/362561

## NVIDIA: DX12 games eventually freeze/crash unless VKD3D shader cache is disabled (VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0)
[...]
- Author: [@AreebDurrani](https://github.com/AreebDurrani)
- State: open
- Created: 2026-01-12T17:31:49Z
- Updated: 2026-01-19T17:20:48Z
[...]
On NVIDIA GPUs, DX12 games using VKD3D-Proton will eventually freeze or crash after some time unless the VKD3D shader cache is disabled via:

VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0

With the shader cache disabled, the freezes/crashes no longer occur (at the cost of increased shader compilation stutter).
[...]
This issue is not related to DLSS or Frame Generation. It reproduces: In games with no DLSS support, whether DLSS is enabled or disabled, and across multiple GPUs
[...]
**Software environment:**

Driver: NVIDIA Linux driver 590.48.01-1

VKD3D-Proton: 3.0b

Wine: 10.20 staging

Kernel: 6.18.3-zen1-1-zen

Session: Wayland

Compositor: KDE
[...]
Start a DX12 game via Proton, play normally. After some amount of time (varies by game; typically minutes to an hour), the game will:
[...]
Freeze permanently, or
[...]
Crash without producing a useful error message
[...]
**Expected behavior:**
[...]
Games should continue running indefinitely without freezing or crashing.
[...]
**Actual behavior:**
Games eventually freeze or crash unless the VKD3D shader cache is disabled.
[...]
**Workaround:**
[...]
Disabling the VKD3D shader cache fully prevents the issue:

VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0

This workaround is reliable across all tested systems and games, but introduces shader compilation stutter.
[...]
This lack of diagnostics is part of what makes the issue difficult to debug, but the shader-cache workaround consistently avoids the problem.
[...]
This issue is independent of DLSS / Frame Generation

It reproduces even in games that do not contain DLSS code paths
[...]
The fact that disabling the shader cache alone resolves the issue suggests a possible interaction between:
[...]
VKD3D pipeline/shader cache handling
[...]
NVIDIA driver behavior (590.xx branch)
[...]
**@OneShot95** commented · Jan 19, 2026 at 5:20pm
[...]
> On Nvidia drivers - 590.48.01, this issue is still present.
Source: NVIDIA: DX12 games eventually freeze/crash unless VKD3D shader cache is disabled (VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0) · Issue #2793 · HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton (https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2793)

## VK_EXT_descriptor_heap (PR #2805) fixes Xid 109 Hard Crashes + Crimson Desert now may work on the 595 drivers for RTX 5000 cards (Blackwell)
[...]
- Author: [@ThomasEricB](https://github.com/ThomasEricB)
- State: open
- Reactions: 👍 4
- Created: 2026-03-27T17:56:04Z
- Updated: 2026-04-02T03:36:32Z
[...]
Crimson Desert completely crashes during loading on Blackwell cards with the latest 595 drivers. @NVIDIA internally tracked the issue as ["6017526 Crimson Desert still soft locks upon completing shader compilation part of loading (field of cubes) with driver 595.58.03"](https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/595-release-feedback-discussion/362561/228).
[...]
HOWEVER, using Proton-CachyOS's compiled https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/pull/2805 ("Prototype implementation of VK_EXT_descriptor_heap") with the flag `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1` **the game now works**! See logs below with the flag and without the flag turned on.
[...]
Also might be the fix for the tree issue as reported by me and others on the Proton Github (I don't know if it is the latest drivers or VK_EXT_descriptor_heap): https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/9595#issuecomment-4141966615 . Tree artifacts completely gone in 595 drivers + VK_EXT_descriptor_heap.
[...]
**EDIT:** There are many reports below + on Reddit and on Discord that using the VK_EXT_descriptor_heap branch also fixes Xid 109 Hard Crashes. Since it has been massively reported to me by many people, I decided to incorporate this into this issue.
[...]
**EDIT 2:** Conversely, there is a minority of people that this workaround didn't work for them in Crimson Desert right away. I suspect that their game may be left in bad state after trying to run the game without this workaround first. All that I can suggest to people who have been struggling is to clean their steam cache and verify their game files. This shader cleanup has been reported to solve these issues with the workaround most of the time. However, If that still doesn't work, all I can think of is to backup their save and reinstall the game. For solving shader issues, I just do (always double check the file paths using `rm -rf` commands!):
[...]
```
rm -rf ~/.cache/nvidia/GLCache/*
rm -rf ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/shadercache/3321460/
rm -rf ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Crimson\ Desert/bin64/vkd3d-proton.cache
rm -rf ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Crimson\ Desert/bin64/vkd3d-proton.cache.write
```
Source: VK_EXT_descriptor_heap (PR #2805) fixes Xid 109 Hard Crashes + Crimson Desert now may work on the 595 drivers for RTX 5000 cards (Blackwell) · Issue #2914 · HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton (https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2914)

Thank you and everyone else who reported the crash with Forza Horizon 6. We have root caused the issue and will ship a fix in an upcoming driver release. The bug for tracking this issue is 6181953.

I was able to get a successful bootup by setting the proton version to experimental with these launch arguments VKD3D_DISABLE_EXTENSIONS=VK_EXT_mesh_shader,VK_NV_raw_access_chains %command%
[...]
50

(no startup)
[...]
Tested with RTX 3080 (starts up fine)
[...]
fresh installation of Windrose (Steam) crashes on startup using nvidia 595.x on an RTX5080. several Proton versions tested.

Downgrading to 580 or 590 fixes the crash and no issues are seen.
[...]
I can’t boot with Kernel 7.0+ with R595 driver (I tried 595.71.05, 595.58.03 and 595.44.07) using the .run file.
[...]
I boot with Kernel 7.0.7 (and previous versions) in multi-user mode (no X environment), installl the driver .run (compile modules, install driver and 32bit libraries, rebuild initramfs) and reboot.
[...]
From now, is impossible boot with Kernel 7.0+. I have the journalctl -b -k. Final lines:
[...]
I attached the full log.

The only fix to my situation is boot with kernel 6.19.12.

## Cyberpunk 2077 - Memory leak to OOM with DXR
[...]
I found that by setting `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr`, Cyberpunk would no longer consume all of system memory and hang the thread on close.
[...]
I was attempting to play Cyberpunk 2077, which I had bought from GOG, via Lutris. Starting the game worked fine and remained relatively stable for a few minutes. A moment later the game would freeze and my whole system with it, until the OOM killer would kick in to start shooting down whatever processes I had open.
[...]
I observed the game's resource usage and saw it would immediately jump to around 6GB RAM usage on the intro, slowly increasing to around 8GB in the main menu, and finally when loading into a save it would be around 10GB from where it would over time increase to fill my full 32GB RAM and 16GB pagefile, freezing the system and activating the OOM killer.
[...]
Eventually I tried different versions of the Lutris runner libraries for Wine, and I found VKD3D 2.10 ran perfectly fine; Cyberpunk launched to around 2GB RAM usage, climbed to 4GB loading into a save, and hovered well under 6GB for hours without issue.
[...]
Checking the release notes for VKD3D 2.11 I saw that DXR became the default option, so I disabled it with the `nodxr` option, and it worked.
[...]
I do not know what that feature is, what it is meant to do or why it causes the game to consume all memory and hang the thread, but I'm quite sure the issue stems from its interaction with something on my system.
[...]
, all current

## VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST in Cyberpunk 2077 photo mode with GPU descriptor heap enabled
[...]
Cyberpunk 2077 hangs with VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST when taking a photo in photo mode while GPU descriptor heap support is enabled via PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1. The game is 100% stable during normal gameplay both with and without the descriptor heap support enabled; the crash is specific to pressing spacebar to capture a photo in photo mode with heap support enabled.
[...]
| Component | Details |
| --- | --- |
| **CPU** | AMD Ryzen 9 9950X 16-Core (32 threads) |
| **GPU** | NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 |
| **Driver** | NVIDIA 595.71.05 |
| **OS** | CachyOS |
| **Kernel** | 7.0.2-2-cachyos (x86_64) |
[...]
1. Install Cyberpunk 2077
2. Use proton-cachyos (tested with `cachyos-10.0-20260420-slr` and `cachyos-11.0-20260428-slr`)
3. Set launch options / environment: `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1`
4. Launch game, load a save, enter photo mode
5. Press spacebar to take a photo
6. Graphics hang immediately — display freezes, audio continues playing
[...]
```
May 02 13:34:54 cachyzen kernel: NVRM: Xid (PCI:0000:01:00): 31, pid=271667, name=Cyberpunk2077.e, channel 0x00000044, intr 00000000. MMU Fault: ENGINE GRAPHICS HUBCLIENT_FE faulted @ 0x0_0aca1000. Fault is of type FAULT_PTE ACCESS_TYPE_VIRT_WRITE
```
[...]
Maybe related: shortly before the crash, the log shows repeated `ClearUnorderedAccessViewUint` calls with heap invalidation warnings:
[...]
```
87018.032: d3d12_command_list_ClearUnorderedAccessViewUint: ClearUAVUint called without active heap.
87018.032: d3d12_command_list_invalidate_descriptor_heap: Invalidating descriptor heap due to meta command which is incompatible with heaps.
```
[...]
Then the hang:
[...]
```
87018.180: d3d12_device_mark_as_removed: Device 0000000129330080 is lost (reason 0x887a0005, "VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST").
87018.180: vkd3d_memory_transfer_queue_flush_locked: Failed to submit command buffer, vr -4.

[...]
Start a DX12 game via Proton, play normally. After some amount of time (varies by game; typically minutes to an hour), the game will:

Freeze permanently, or
[...]
Crash without producing a useful error message

Games should continue running indefinitely without freezing or crashing.
[...]

Games eventually freeze or crash unless the VKD3D shader cache is disabled.
[...]

Disabling the VKD3D shader cache fully prevents the issue:

[...]
This issue is independent of DLSS / Frame Generation

It reproduces even in games that do not contain DLSS code paths

VKD3D pipeline/shader cache handling

summary.text:
# NVIDIA Driver Stability Issues with VKD3D-Proton (2025-2026)

## Overview
Multiple critical stability issues affect DX12 games running through VKD3D-Proton on NVIDIA GPUs, particularly with driver versions 580.x, 590.x, and 595.x. Issues range from device loss crashes to system-wide freezes.

## Key Issues

### 1. **VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST Crashes (Multiple Driver Versions)**
- **Affected:** Cyberpunk 2077, other DX12 games
- **Drivers:** 580.x, 595.x
- **Symptom:** Game freezes/crashes after 5-45 minutes (Cyberpunk); system becomes unresponsive; GPU utilization drops to 1%
- **Root cause:** Related to descriptor heap pressure and view map recreation; occurs at Vulkan userspace level with no kernel errors reported
- **Status:** Issue #2756 (open, reported 2025-12-16)

### 2. **DX12 Shader Cache Freeze/Crash Bug (590.x)**
- **Affected:** Multiple DX12 titles, including non-DLSS games
- **Drivers:** 590.48.01 confirmed; affects 590.xx branch
- **Symptom:** Games freeze or crash after variable playtime (minutes to hours)
- **Workaround:** `VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0` (disables crash but increases shader compilation stutter)
- **Status:** Issue #2793 (open, reported 2026-01-12)
- **Note:** Suggests interaction between VKD3D cache handling and NVIDIA driver behavior

### 3. **Xid 109 Hard Crashes & Game Load Failures (595.x)**
- **Affected:** Crimson Desert (Blackwell/RTX 5000 cards), other titles
- **Drivers:** 595.x (595.58.03, 595.71.05 confirmed)
- **Symptom:** Hard crashes during shader compilation or gameplay; soft locks
- **Potential fix:** `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1` flag with VK_EXT_descriptor_heap implementation (PR #2805)
- **Status:** Widely reported; fix in testing phase
- **Caveat:** Some users report cache corruption requiring shader cleanup

### 4. **Cyberpunk 2077 DXR Memory Leak**
- **Drivers:** Affects VKD3D 2.11+ (DXR enabled by default)
- **Symptom:** System memory consumption climbs to 32GB+, triggering OOM killer; system freeze
- **Workaround:** `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr` resolves the issue
- **Status:** Issue #2549 (open)

### 5. **Photo Mode Hang (595.x with Descriptor Heap)**
- **Affected:** Cyberpunk 2077
- **Drivers:** 595.71.05
- **Symptom:** VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST when taking photos in photo mode with `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1`
- **Status:** Issue #2979 (open, reported 2026-05-02)

## Recommended Workarounds (By Symptom)

| Issue | Workaround | Trade-offs |
|-------|-----------|-----------|
| General DX12 freezes (590.x) | `VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0` | Shader compilation stutter |
| Xid 109 crashes (595.x) | `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1` | May break photo mode; cache corruption possible |
| DXR memory leak | `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr` | Disables ray tracing features |
| Descriptor heap issues | `VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv` | Limited effectiveness (~45min extension in Cyberpunk) |
| Specific Subnautica 2 crash (RTX 50-series) | `VKD3D_DISABLE_EXTENSIONS=VK_EXT_mesh_shader,VK_NV_raw_access_chains` | Feature degradation |

## Caveats & Unresolved Items

- **Hardware stability confirmed:** Stress tests show GPU is stable; issues are driver/VKD3D interaction
- **Multiple unresolved paths:** No single fix works across all affected games/configurations
- **Descriptor heap workaround side effects:** Some users report it fixes some crashes but breaks others (photo mode)
- **Kernel compatibility:** 595.x drivers have boot issues with Kernel 7.0+ on some systems; Kernel 6.19.x workaround reported
- **Shader cache cleanup needed:** Using descriptor heap workaround may require manual cache clearing (`~/.cache/nvidia/GLCache/*`, Steam shadercache)

## Sources

1. [Cyberpunk 2077] VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after view map pressure on NVIDIA 580.x — https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2756
2. NVIDIA: DX12 games eventually freeze/crash unless VKD3D shader cache is disabled — https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2793
3. VK_EXT_descriptor_heap (PR #2805) fixes Xid 109 Hard Crashes — https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2914
4. Cyberpunk 2077 - Memory leak to OOM with DXR — https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2549
5. VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST in Cyberpunk 2077 photo mode with GPU descriptor heap enabled — https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2979
6. NVIDIA 595 Drivers release feedback & discussion — https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/595-release-feedback-discussion/362561

Okay, now I have a clear picture. Here's what's going on:

---

## Your System
- **GPU:** RTX 2070 SUPER (8GB VRAM)
- **NVIDIA driver:** 595.71.05 (open kernel module)
- **Kernel:** 7.0.9-arch2-1
- **Proton:** GE-Proton10-34
- **Current launch options:** `DXVK_NVAPI_DISABLE_LOW_LATENCY=1 VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr gamemoderun %command% --launcher-skip`

## The Problem

This is a **known, active bug** upstream — specifically [vkd3d-proton#2756](https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2756) and [#2793](https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2793). It's **not** a compositor issue.

The NVIDIA 595.x drivers have a VKD3D interaction bug that causes `VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST` under descriptor heap pressure. Cyberpunk's inventory/map UI triggers "intense view map recreation" which hits this path. GPU drops from full utilization to ~1%, game sound continues, but the display is frozen — exactly your symptom.

You already have `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr` which disables DXR (good — avoids the separate DXR memory leak). But the **freeze on overlay/menu open** is a different bugpath.

## Recommended Launch Options

Try this, ordered by likelihood of fixing it:

### 1. Nuclear option — disable shader cache (most reported success)
```
VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0 DXVK_NVAPI_DISABLE_LOW_LATENCY=1 gamemoderun %command% --launcher-skip
```
`VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0` disables the VKD3D shader cache, which is the **#1 confirmed workaround** for the freeze-on-overlay bug on NVIDIA 590.x/595.x. The tradeoff: you'll get shader compilation stutter the first time new effects appear, but it should stop the freeze.

### 2. If #1 works but stutter is annoying, try adding the heap flag
```
VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0 DXVK_NVAPI_DISABLE_LOW_LATENCY=1 PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1 gamemoderun %command% --launcher-skip
```
`PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1` enables the experimental descriptor heap path which *may* work better on your driver — but there's a reported bug where **photo mode hangs** with this enabled on 595.x (#2979). Test if you use photo mode.

### 3. If nothing helps — Gamescope as a last resort
```
VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr DXVK_NVAPI_DISABLE_LOW_LATENCY=1 gamescope -W 2560 -H 1440 -f -- %command% --launcher-skip
```
Adjust resolution to your monitor. Gamescope isolates the game's present path from the desktop compositor and can sidestep the VKD3D swapchain stall entirely.

---

**Start with option 1** — `VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0` is the most widely confirmed fix for the exact freeze pattern you're seeing on the 595 driver branch. The stutter tradeoff is annoying at first but gets better as shaders compile, and it beats hard-freeeing every time you open your map.

Want me to also check if there's a newer GE-Proton or if you should try `proton-cachyos` which some ProtonDB reporters prefer?
Okay, I tried uh the first Um option. And it worked great for about twenty seconds. Then I opened A map. Again. Which worked fine on the first open. Uh but then it froze again

content.thinking:
The first workaround didn't work. The shader cache disable didn't prevent the freeze. Let me think about what's happening:

1. `VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0` didn't help - so it's not the shader cache path bug (#2793)
2. `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr` was already set
3. The freeze happens when opening the map/inventory a second time

This points more directly at the descriptor heap / view map recreation issue (#2756). The "intense view map pressure" bug. On the second overlay open, VKD3D tries to recreate view maps (2048 → 3072 or similar), and this triggers VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST on NVIDIA 595.x.

Let me check if there are more specific workarounds, particularly around the `no_upload_hvv` flag or the Gamescope approach. Also, the user is on an RTX 2070 SUPER with only 8GB VRAM - that might be relevant too.

Actually, let me also check whether `VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv` was mentioned. That was noted as extending crash time from 5min to ~45min in the issue tracker, but the user's freeze happens almost immediately on second map open.

Let me search for more specific fixes and also check if the user should try Gamescope (which bypasses the desktop compositor entirely) or if there's a more targeted VKD3D flag.

Also worth checking: is the user on Wayland or X11? That matters for the presentpath.

content.text: OK so shader cache wasn't the culprit. The fact that it works on first open but freezes on the second maps directly to the **view map recreation pressure** bug (#2756) — VKD3D reallocates descriptor resources on repeated overlay opens and the 595.x driver chokes.

## Cyberpunk 2077 - Memory leak to OOM with DXR
[...]
I found that by setting `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr`, Cyberpunk would no longer consume all of system memory and hang the thread on close.
[...]
I was attempting to play Cyberpunk 2077, which I had bought from GOG, via Lutris. Starting the game worked fine and remained relatively stable for a few minutes. A moment later the game would freeze and my whole system with it, until the OOM killer would kick in to start shooting down whatever processes I had open.
[...]
I observed the game's resource usage and saw it would immediately jump to around 6GB RAM usage on the intro, slowly increasing to around 8GB in the main menu, and finally when loading into a save it would be around 10GB from where it would over time increase to fill my full 32GB RAM and 16GB pagefile, freezing the system and activating the OOM killer.
[...]
(de)activating some
[...]
features and changing game settings.
[...]
None of those had an
[...]
Eventually I tried different versions of the Lutris runner libraries for Wine, and I found VKD3D 2.10 ran perfectly fine; Cyberpunk launched to around 2GB RAM usage, climbed to 4GB loading into a save, and hovered well under 6GB for hours without issue.
[...]
Checking the release notes for VKD3D 2.11 I saw that DXR became the default option, so I disabled it with the `nodxr` option, and it worked.
[...]
I do not know what that feature is, what it is meant to do or why it causes the game to consume all memory and hang the thread, but I'm quite sure the issue stems from its interaction with something on my system.
[...]
- GPU: Intel B580
- Driver: mesa-tkg-git 25.2.0_devel.208628.3c7a834ebc7-1 with KMS xe driver
- Wine version: GE-Proton and Wine versions up to Wine 10.8 that I tried
- VKD3D-Proton version: 2.14, 2.13, 2.12, 2.11.1, 2.11 with DXR on by default
[...]

## [Cyberpunk 2077] VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after view map pressure on NVIDIA 580.x
[...]
Cyberpunk 2077 crashes consistently with VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST on NVIDIA 580.x driver after 5-45 minutes of gameplay. The crash appears related to descriptor heap pressure ("Intense view map pressure" in logs). Hardware is confirmed stable via stress testing. Multiple workarounds attempted without success.
[...]
- Arch Linux, kernel 6.17.9-zen
- NVIDIA RTX 2080, driver 580.105.08
- Ryzen 9 9950X3D, 96GB RAM
- Proton Experimental / GE-Proton (both affected)
[...]
Game freezes with VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after variable playtime (5-45 minutes depending on settings). GPU utilization drops from 99% to 1%.
[...]
shows

Intense view map pressure, recreating view maps (2048 -> 3072)
vkd3d_queue_wait_idle: vkQueueWaitIdle failed, vr -4 (VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST)
[...]
### Tested workarounds (all still crash eventually):
[...]
- VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv (best result, ~45min before crash)
- VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_0
- PROTON_HEAP_DELAY_FREE=1
- PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=0
- Ray Tracing OFF, lowest graphics settings
- Driver downgrade failed (DKMS incompatible with kernel 6.17).
[...]
**GPU stress test**: gpu-burn 5 minutes, 0 errors - hardware is stable.
[...]
> > Intense view map pressure
> 
> This is completely benign. And 3k is nothing here. It's expected when RT is enabled.
[...]
> Looking at the logs more closely, the crash happens ~14 seconds after "Intense view map pressure! Got 5120 views". Between the warning and the crash, there's no other error - just QueryInterface warnings which seem unrelated.
> 
> Timeline:
> 
> - 1773.845: "Intense view map pressure! Got 5120 views"
> - 1788.417: "Failed to submit queue(s), vr -4" → VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST
> 
> This was with RT OFF and lowest settings. Is 5120 views still considered normal?
> 
> I can provide dmesg/kernel logs from the crash if that would help identify the driver-side failure.
[...]
> Additional findings from further testing:
> 
> ### No kernel errors
> 
> No kernel/dmesg errors during the crash. The GPU device lost happens purely at Vulkan/userspace level - the NVIDIA kernel driver (580.105.08) doesn't report any fault or timeout.
> 
> ### System-wide freeze
> 
> The freeze affects the entire desktop, not just the game process. Mouse/keyboard become unresponsive fo

don't

is a VK

Today, Cyberpunk suddenly stopped running, and consistently crashes inside the nvidia driver. Everything worked yesterday, and I have verified that no relevant system level packages updates took place since the last time I ran the game. This is the same nvidia driver, kernel, mesa, etc that worked yesterday.

## NVIDIA: DX12 games eventually freeze/crash unless VKD3D shader cache is disabled (VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0)
[...]
On NVIDIA GPUs, DX12 games using VKD3D-Proton will eventually freeze or crash after some time unless the VKD3D shader cache is disabled via:

VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0

With the shader cache disabled, the freezes/crashes no longer occur (at the cost of increased shader compilation stutter).
[...]
This issue is not related to DLSS or Frame Generation. It reproduces: In games with no DLSS support, whether DLSS is enabled or disabled, and across multiple GPUs
[...]
**Software environment:**

Driver: NVIDIA Linux driver 590.48.01-1

VKD3D-Proton: 3.0b

Wine: 10.20 staging

Kernel: 6.18.3-zen1-1-zen

Session: Wayland

Compositor: KDE
[...]
This occurs in multiple DX12 titles, including games that do not support DLSS at all.
[...]
**Reproduction behavior:**
[...]
Start a DX12 game via Proton, play normally. After some amount of time (varies by game; typically minutes to an hour), the game will:

Freeze permanently, or
[...]
Crash without producing a useful error message
[...]
**Expected behavior:**
Games should continue running indefinitely without freezing or crashing.
[...]
**Actual behavior:**
Games eventually freeze or crash unless the VKD3D shader cache is disabled.
[...]
**Workaround:**
Disabling the VKD3D shader cache fully prevents the issue:

VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0
[...]
This workaround is reliable across all tested systems and games, but introduces shader compilation stutter.
[...]
This lack of diagnostics is
[...]
makes the issue
[...]
This issue is independent of DLSS / Frame Generation

It reproduces even in games that do not contain DLSS code paths
[...]
The fact that disabling the shader cache alone resolves the issue suggests a possible interaction between:

VKD3D pipeline/shader cache handling
[...]
NVIDIA driver behavior (590.xx branch)
[...]
> On Nvidia drivers - 590.48.01, this issue is still present.

Source: KCD2: GPU VA space exhaustion on RTX 5060 (Blackwell) - intermapRegisterDmaMapping failures causing freeze after ~1hr · Issue #2876 · HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton (https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2876)

This pulls in the rest of descriptor heap, since it's only reasonable way right now to get working benchmark without rendering glitches on my system. At best, this can be a hotfix branch or something.
[...]
After that, we have the "lovely" bug of bad aliasing between image descriptors and buffer descriptors. The game first writes an image descriptor, then reads that as a plain buffer, which is broken, and on RDNA3/4 we would read the image descriptor at a 16b sliced offset, which breaks. This is the first instance of this causing issues in the wild. It was a known hazard point, but we have easy mitigation strategies on AMD at least.
[...]
Fixing this for descriptor buffer is very hard since it means rejigging new fast paths for what is supposed to be a really terrible workaround. The workaround for now only applies to descriptor heap since it was quite easy to do.
[...]
There are also bugs with "use-before-alloc" where the application allocates resources after submit and writing descriptors pointing to them to the descriptor heap while GPU is in flight. Utter insanity, but FH5 had issues in this area too.
[...]
It's not been proven to cause a hang yet I think, but `radv_wait_for_vm_map_updates=true` is very likely going to be needed for safety.

`RADV_EXPERIMENTAL=heap` is also needed since descriptor heap is not enabled by default.
[...]
Also fixes a silly bug that happened with RDNA2 descriptor heap while dealing with reviews, but that's not really relevant to this PR.
[...]
- This PR
- Use Mesa git
- `RADV_EXPERIMENTAL=heap radv_wait_for_vm_map_updates=true %command%`

content.text:
# VKD3D-Proton Stability Issues: Summary & Workarounds

## Key Issues

### 1. **NVIDIA: Shader Cache Freeze/Crash**
On NVIDIA GPUs, DX12 games using VKD3D-Proton frequently freeze or crash after minutes to hours of play unless the shader cache is disabled.

**Workaround:**
```
VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0
```
- Reliably prevents freezes across all tested systems
- Trade-off: Increases shader compilation stutter
- Affects NVIDIA drivers 590.x+ and is independent of DLSS/Frame Generation
- Reproduces even in games without DLSS support

### 2. **Cyberpunk 2077: VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST on NVIDIA 580.x**
Game crashes with `VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST` after 5-45 minutes of gameplay, often triggered by high descriptor heap pressure ("Intense view map pressure").

**Tested workarounds (limited effectiveness):**
- `VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv` — best result but still crashes after ~45 min
- `VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_0`
- `PROTON_HEAP_DELAY_FREE=1`
- `PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=0`
- Ray tracing disabled + lowest graphics

**Note:** Hardware stress tests confirm GPU stability; issue appears to be driver-level, not hardware failure.

### 3. **Photo Mode Hang with GPU Descriptor Heap**
Cyberpunk 2077 hangs with `VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST` when taking photos with `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1` enabled. Occurs during `ClearUnorderedAccessViewUint` calls with heap invalidation warnings.

### 4. **AMD: Death Stranding Crash (Regression in 2.15+)**
Death Stranding crashes within 1-2 minutes on AMD GPUs with current VKD3D-Proton. Reverts to 2.14.1 resolve the issue. Users report `no_upload_hvv` workaround causes full system crashes on some AMD cards (9070XT).

### 5. **Cyberpunk 2077: Memory Leak with DXR**
Game consumes all available RAM (32GB+ systems) with DXR enabled, freezing the system. Fixed by Intel iGPU users by disabling DXR.

**Workaround:**
```
VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr
```
- Issue introduced in VKD3D 2.11 when DXR became default
- Works fine with VKD3D 2.10 and earlier

## General Caveats

- **Descriptor heap is experimental:** The `VK_EXT_descriptor_heap` implementation is still in prototype stage with known bugs (aliasing issues, use-before-alloc hazards).
- **Driver-level issues:** Many crashes occur at the Vulkan/userspace level with no kernel errors logged, suggesting NVIDIA driver bugs rather than VKD3D issues.
- **Game-specific:** Stability varies significantly by title and GPU vendor; workarounds may help one game but not another.

---

## Sources

1. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2793 — NVIDIA shader cache freeze
2. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2756 — Cyberpunk NVIDIA 580.x crash
3. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2979 — Photo mode descriptor heap hang
4. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2829 — Death Stranding AMD regression
5. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2549 — Cyberpunk DXR memory leak

## NVIDIA: DX12 games eventually freeze/crash unless VKD3D shader cache is disabled (VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0)
[...]
On NVIDIA GPUs, DX12 games using VKD3D-Proton will eventually freeze or crash after some time unless the VKD3D shader cache is disabled via:

VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0
[...]
With the shader cache disabled, the freezes/crashes no longer occur (at the cost of increased shader compilation stutter).
[...]
This issue is not related to DLSS or Frame Generation. It reproduces: In games with no DLSS support, whether DLSS is enabled or disabled, and across multiple GPUs
[...]
Start a DX12 game via Proton, play normally. After some amount of time (varies by game; typically minutes to an hour), the game will:
[...]
Freeze permanently, or
[...]
Crash without producing a useful error message
[...]
**Expected behavior:**
[...]
Games should continue running indefinitely without freezing or crashing.
[...]
**Actual behavior:**
Games eventually freeze or crash unless the VKD3D shader cache is disabled.
[...]
**Workaround:**
Disabling the VKD3D shader cache fully prevents the issue:

VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0

This workaround is reliable across all tested systems and games, but introduces shader compilation stutter.
[...]
This lack of diagnostics is part of what makes the issue difficult to debug, but the shader-cache workaround consistently avoids the problem.
[...]
This issue is independent of DLSS / Frame Generation

It reproduces even in games that do not contain DLSS code paths
[...]
The fact that disabling the shader cache alone resolves the issue suggests a possible interaction between:

VKD3D pipeline/shader cache handling
[...]
> On Nvidia drivers - 590.48.01, this issue is still present.
Source: NVIDIA: DX12 games eventually freeze/crash unless VKD3D shader cache is disabled (VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0) · Issue #2793 · HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton (https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2793)

## Cyberpunk 2077 - Memory leak to OOM with DXR
[...]
I found that by setting `VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr`, Cyberpunk would no longer consume all of system memory and hang the thread on close.
[...]
I was attempting to play Cyberpunk 2077, which I had bought from GOG, via Lutris. Starting the game worked fine and remained relatively stable for a few minutes. A moment later the game would freeze and my whole system with it, until the OOM killer would kick in to start shooting down whatever processes I had open.
[...]
I observed the game's resource usage and saw it would immediately jump to around 6GB RAM usage on the intro, slowly increasing to around 8GB in the main menu, and finally when loading into a save it would be around 10GB from where it would over time increase to fill my full 32GB RAM and 16GB pagefile, freezing the system and activating the OOM killer.
[...]
(de)activating some
[...]
features and changing game settings.
[...]
None of those had an
[...]
Eventually I tried different versions of the Lutris runner libraries for Wine, and I found VKD3D 2.10 ran perfectly fine; Cyberpunk launched to around 2GB RAM usage, climbed to 4GB loading into a save, and hovered well under 6GB for hours without issue.
[...]
Checking the release notes for VKD3D 2.11 I saw that DXR became the default option, so I disabled it with the `nodxr` option, and it worked.
[...]
I do not know what that feature is, what it is meant to do or why it causes the game to consume all memory and hang the thread, but I'm quite sure the issue stems from its interaction with something on my system.
[...]
- GPU: Intel B580
- Driver: mesa-tkg-git 25.2.0_devel.208628.3c7a834ebc7-1 with KMS xe driver
- Wine version: GE-Proton and Wine versions up to Wine 10.8 that I tried
- VKD3D-Proton version: 2.14, 2.13, 2.12, 2.11.1, 2.11 with DXR on by default
[...]
[dxr_ge-proton-10.10_logs.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21335961/dxr_ge-proton-10.10_logs.tar.gz)
This first is with DXR enabled, few minutes in-game, and having to SIGKILL the process after quitting.

## [Cyberpunk 2077] VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after view map pressure on NVIDIA 580.x
[...]
Cyberpunk 2077 crashes consistently with VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST on NVIDIA 580.x driver after 5-45 minutes of gameplay. The crash appears related to descriptor heap pressure ("Intense view map pressure" in logs). Hardware is confirmed stable via stress testing. Multiple workarounds attempted without success.
[...]
- Arch Linux, kernel 6.17.9-zen
- NVIDIA RTX 2080, driver 580.105.08
- Ryzen 9 9950X3D, 96GB RAM
- Proton Experimental / GE-Proton (both affected)
[...]
Game freezes with VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after variable playtime (5-45 minutes depending on settings). GPU utilization drops from 99% to 1%.
[...]
shows

Intense view map pressure, recreating view maps (2048 -> 3072)
vkd3d_queue_wait_idle: vkQueueWaitIdle failed, vr -4 (VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST)
[...]
### Tested workarounds (all still crash eventually):
[...]
- VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv (best result, ~45min before crash)
- VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_0
- PROTON_HEAP_DELAY_FREE=1
- PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=0
- Ray Tracing OFF, lowest graphics settings
- Driver downgrade failed (DKMS incompatible with kernel 6.17).
[...]
**GPU stress test**: gpu-burn 5 minutes, 0 errors - hardware is stable.
[...]
> > Intense view map pressure
> 
> This is completely benign. And 3k is nothing here. It's expected when RT is enabled.
[...]
> Looking at the logs more closely, the crash happens ~14 seconds after "Intense view map pressure! Got 5120 views". Between the warning and the crash, there's no other error - just QueryInterface warnings which seem unrelated.
> 
> Timeline:
> 
> - 1773.845: "Intense view map pressure! Got 5120 views"
> - 1788.417: "Failed to submit queue(s), vr -4" → VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST
> 
> This was with RT OFF and lowest settings. Is 5120 views still considered normal?
> 
> I can provide dmesg/kernel logs from the crash if that would help identify the driver-side failure.
[...]
> Additional findings from further testing:
> 
> ### No kernel errors
> 
> No kernel/dmesg errors during the crash. The GPU device lost happens purely at Vulkan/userspace level - the NVIDIA kernel driver (580.105.08) doesn't report any fault or timeout.
> 
> ### System-wide freeze
> 
> The freeze affects the entire desktop, not just the game process. Mouse/keyboard become unresponsive fo

hangs waiting 
for
[...]
work that can never complete
[...]
## VKD3D_CONFIG used
[...]
```
VKD3D_CONFIG=no_dynamic_resources,no_upload_hvv,force_host_cached
```
[...]
force_host_cached was added specifically to target ErrorCode 0x0
Source: KCD2: GPU VA space exhaustion on RTX 5060 (Blackwell) - intermapRegisterDmaMapping failures causing freeze after ~1hr · Issue #2876 · HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton (https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2876)

This pulls in the rest of descriptor heap, since it's only reasonable way right now to get working benchmark without rendering glitches on my system. At best, this can be a hotfix branch or something.
[...]
After that, we have the "lovely" bug of bad aliasing between image descriptors and buffer descriptors. The game first writes an image descriptor, then reads that as a plain buffer, which is broken, and on RDNA3/4 we would read the image descriptor at a 16b sliced offset, which breaks. This is the first instance of this causing issues in the wild. It was a known hazard point, but we have easy mitigation strategies on AMD at least.
[...]
Fixing this for descriptor buffer is very hard since it means rejigging new fast paths for what is supposed to be a really terrible workaround. The workaround for now only applies to descriptor heap since it was quite easy to do.
[...]
There are also bugs with "use-before-alloc" where the application allocates resources after submit and writing descriptors pointing to them to the descriptor heap while GPU is in flight. Utter insanity, but FH5 had issues in this area too.
[...]
It's not been proven to cause a hang yet I think, but `radv_wait_for_vm_map_updates=true` is very likely going to be needed for safety.

`RADV_EXPERIMENTAL=heap` is also needed since descriptor heap is not enabled by default.
[...]
Also fixes a silly bug that happened with RDNA2 descriptor heap while dealing with reviews, but that's not really relevant to this PR.
[...]
- This PR
- Use Mesa git
- `RADV_EXPERIMENTAL=heap radv_wait_for_vm_map_updates=true %command%`

summary.text:
# VKD3D-Proton Stability Issues: Summary & Workarounds

## Key Issues

### 1. **NVIDIA: Shader Cache Freeze/Crash**
On NVIDIA GPUs, DX12 games using VKD3D-Proton frequently freeze or crash after minutes to hours of play unless the shader cache is disabled.

**Workaround:**
```
VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH=0
```
- Reliably prevents freezes across all tested systems
- Trade-off: Increases shader compilation stutter
- Affects NVIDIA drivers 590.x+ and is independent of DLSS/Frame Generation
- Reproduces even in games without DLSS support

### 2. **Cyberpunk 2077: VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST on NVIDIA 580.x**
Game crashes with `VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST` after 5-45 minutes of gameplay, often triggered by high descriptor heap pressure ("Intense view map pressure").

**Tested workarounds (limited effectiveness):**
- `VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv` — best result but still crashes after ~45 min
- `VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_0`
- `PROTON_HEAP_DELAY_FREE=1`
- `PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=0`
- Ray tracing disabled + lowest graphics

**Note:** Hardware stress tests confirm GPU stability; issue appears to be driver-level, not hardware failure.

### 3. **Photo Mode Hang with GPU Descriptor Heap**
Cyberpunk 2077 hangs with `VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST` when taking photos with `PROTON_VKD3D_HEAP=1` enabled. Occurs during `ClearUnorderedAccessViewUint` calls with heap invalidation warnings.

### 4. **AMD: Death Stranding Crash (Regression in 2.15+)**
Death Stranding crashes within 1-2 minutes on AMD GPUs with current VKD3D-Proton. Reverts to 2.14.1 resolve the issue. Users report `no_upload_hvv` workaround causes full system crashes on some AMD cards (9070XT).

### 5. **Cyberpunk 2077: Memory Leak with DXR**
Game consumes all available RAM (32GB+ systems) with DXR enabled, freezing the system. Fixed by Intel iGPU users by disabling DXR.

**Workaround:**
```
VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr
```
- Issue introduced in VKD3D 2.11 when DXR became default
- Works fine with VKD3D 2.10 and earlier

## General Caveats

- **Descriptor heap is experimental:** The `VK_EXT_descriptor_heap` implementation is still in prototype stage with known bugs (aliasing issues, use-before-alloc hazards).
- **Driver-level issues:** Many crashes occur at the Vulkan/userspace level with no kernel errors logged, suggesting NVIDIA driver bugs rather than VKD3D issues.
- **Game-specific:** Stability varies significantly by title and GPU vendor; workarounds may help one game but not another.

---

## Sources

1. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2793 — NVIDIA shader cache freeze
2. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2756 — Cyberpunk NVIDIA 580.x crash
3. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2979 — Photo mode descriptor heap hang
4. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2829 — Death Stranding AMD regression
5. https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/issues/2549 — Cyberpunk DXR memory leak

urls.title: [Cyberpunk 2077] VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST after view map pressure on NVIDIA 580.x
urls.content:
Cyberpunk 2077 crashes consistently with VK\_ERROR\_DEVICE\_LOST on NVIDIA 580.x driver after 5-45 minutes of gameplay. The crash appears related to descriptor heap pressure ("Intense view map pressure" in logs). Hardware is confirmed stable via stress testing. Multiple workarounds attempted without success.

### Software information

Cyberpunk 2077 (Steam, AppID 1091500)

### System information

*   Arch Linux, kernel 6.17.9-zen
*   NVIDIA RTX 2080, driver 580.105.08
*   Ryzen 9 9950X3D, 96GB RAM
*   Proton Experimental / GE-Proton (both affected)

### Problem

Game freezes with VK\_ERROR\_DEVICE\_LOST after variable playtime (5-45 minutes depending on settings). GPU utilization drops from 99% to 1%.

### Log shows

Intense view map pressure, recreating view maps (2048 -> 3072)  
vkd3d\_queue\_wait\_idle: vkQueueWaitIdle failed, vr -4 (VK\_ERROR\_DEVICE\_LOST)

### Tested workarounds (all still crash eventually):

*   VKD3D\_CONFIG=no\_upload\_hvv (best result, ~45min before crash)
*   VKD3D\_FEATURE\_LEVEL=12\_0
*   PROTON\_HEAP\_DELAY\_FREE=1
*   PROTON\_ENABLE\_NVAPI=0
*   Ray Tracing OFF, lowest graphics settings
*   Driver downgrade failed (DKMS incompatible with kernel 6.17).

**GPU stress test**: gpu-burn 5 minutes, 0 errors - hardware is stable.

[steam-1091500.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24190749/steam-1091500.log)

it seems it does not really

ery - Dec

## HDR broken using gamescope 3.16.17 on KDE Plasma 6.5.3 on Arch Linux
[...]
The HDR functionality of gamescope broke because of an update of KDE Plasma on Arch Linux.

The current version of gamescope that is delivered on Arch Linux is gamescope 3.16.17-1 updated on 2025-09-08.
The current version of KDE Plasma on Arch Linux is 6.5.3, updated on 2025-12-09.

There was a change in the code of KDE Plasma that handles HDR some time in the last two months. Since then the old version of gamescope (2025-09-08) is not compatible anymore to use the HDR functionality.

The same developer that changed the code in Plasma also made gamescope compatible again by this commit: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/gamescope/commit/7d4e835a25cc85017380cbd743d517b48932fb75
[...]
Unfortunately there was no update from gamescope since 2025-09-08, so Arch Linux users now have a broken HDR functionality until gamescope releases a new update or the package on the Arch Repos gets a hotfix update to include the commit mentioned above manually.
[...]
A rollback of KDE Plasma to something like 6.5.0 is no viable option to many users as it affects many packages and dependencies. A rollback of gamescope is useless, as the incompatibility didn't come from the change in gamescope, but a change in KDE Plasma.
[...]
1) Start a Wayland Session of KDE Plasma 6.5.3 on Arch Linux
2) Install the newest gamescope version
3) Try to use the --hdr-enabled flag when starting gamescope
[...]
--> HDR does not work. It displays SDR and settings menus in games show HDR als "unavailable".
[...]
```markdown
- Distro: Arch Linux x86_64
- CPU: AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor
- GPU: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. [AMD/ATI] Navi 21 [Radeon RX 6800/6800 XT / 6900 XT]
- Driver Version: Mesa 25.3.1-arch1.2
```
[...]
```markdown
- Desktop environment: KDE Plasma 6.5.3
- Session type: Wayland
- Gamescope version: 3.16.17
- Gamescope launch command(s), generated by Lutris, starting Cyberpunk 2077, GOG version:
[...]
gamescope -w 3840 -h 2160 -W 3840 -H 2160 -f --hdr-enabled --force-grab-cursor -- gamemoderun /home/user/.local/share/lutris/runners/wine/wine-ge-8-26-x86_64/bin/wine /home/user/games/gog/cyberpunk-2077/drive_c/GOG Games/Cyberpunk 2077/bin/x64/Cyberpunk2077.exe
```
Source: HDR broken using gamescope 3.16.17 on KDE Plasma 6.5.3 on Arch Linux · Issue #2037 · ValveSoftware/gamescope (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/gamescope/issues/2037)

This fork extends [Valve’s gamescope](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/gamescope) with fixes for **NVIDIA proprietary drivers** on Linux, with emphasis on **RTX 40/50 (Blackwell)** and **driver 570+**, especially **hybrid laptops** (NVIDIA dGPU + integrated GPU running the desktop compositor).
[...]
**Branch with the NVIDIA work:** `feat/nvidia-blackwell-explicit-sync`
[...]
### What the NVIDIA patches change
[...]
- **Nested Wayland + NVIDIA:** If `/dev/nvidiactl` exists and you run under a Wayland session with `--backend auto` (default), gamescope selects the **SDL** backend instead of the Wayland backend. The Wayland backend shares output via DMA-BUF to the parent compositor, which often **fails on hybrid NVIDIA setups** (invalid `wl_buffer` / broken pipe).
[...]
- **Vulkan:** Detects `VK_DRIVER_ID_NVIDIA_PROPRIETARY`, skips Mesa-only paths (e.g. certain WSI memory import), and applies safer defaults for flippable images and sync.
[...]
- **DRM / KMS:** Disables explicit sync by default on NVIDIA (implicit sync fallback); optionally enables an experimental **SYNC_FD** path via ConVar `drm_nvidia_explicit_sync_via_sync_fd`. Proactively disables `IN_FENCE_FD` where it causes atomic commit failures.
[...]
- **Wayland backend:** If you force `--backend wayland`, DMA-BUF modifiers are checked before `create_immed` to avoid fatal protocol errors; explicit sync is not advertised for NVIDIA clients the same way as on Mesa.
[...]
- **Preemptive upscale / timelines:** NVIDIA-specific semaphore bridging and audited error handling (no silent GPU sync skips, FD leaks fixed).
[...]
**Tested by the maintainer:** RTX 5050 Laptop GPU, driver 570, Pop!_OS 24.04 (Wayland), including real games via Steam/Proton.
[...]
**Not a guarantee** for every NVIDIA GPU, every driver version, or nouveau. Reports with `nvidia-smi`, distro, and desktop help narrow issues.
[...]
In an embedded session, gamescope can flip game frames with DRM/KMS with minimal copying. When nested on a normal desktop:
Source: barramee27/gamescope (https://github.com/barramee27/gamescope)
